claude_reasoning.reasoning: Q1 2025 shows zero revenue but significant capex (11,000), indicating construction/pre-operational phase. Missing DSCR calculation is acceptable when no debt service exists.
Q1 2025 shows opex of 11,000 exceeding zero revenue, but this appears to be misclassified capex during construction phase.
Sign convention error - manufacturing overhead should typically be negative (expense) in financial models, not positive.
Sign convention error - capex should typically be negative (cash outflow) in financial models.
Sign convention error - opex should typically be negative (expense) in financial models.
Accounts payable cannot be negative (-25,854). This likely represents a data extraction or calculation error.
AR of 1,850 equals product revenue, suggesting 100% of quarterly revenue remains uncollected. Could be legitimate for project finance with specific payment terms.
DSCR of 19x is unusually high but mathematically possible with low debt service. May indicate missing debt or incorrect DSCR calculation.
Sign convention error - material costs should be negative (expense).
Sign convention error - labor costs should be negative (expense).
Sign convention error - manufacturing overhead should be negative (expense).
Sign convention error - COGS should be negative (expense).
Sign convention error - SG&A should be negative (expense).
Sign convention error - depreciation should be negative (expense).
Sign convention error - interest expense should be negative (expense).
Sign convention error - tax expense should be negative (expense).
Accounts payable should be positive (liability). Negative value indicates extraction error.
